ensemble: 2x Opus 4.8 (inside/outside) + 2x Codex gpt-5.5 xhigh (inside/outside); arbiter Opus 4.8

items.title: Will Graham Platner drop out of the 2026 Maine US Senate election before Jul 14, 2026?
items.rationale: Core: sexual-assault allegation (Politico, 07-06) triggered total party-leadership abandonment (Schumer+Gillibrand+DSCC funding cutoff, endorsers pulling) with a hard Maine replacement deadline (07-13 5pm) inside the window. Risk: Platner ran as anti-establishment populist, forcefully denied, and is 'reflecting' not withdrawing. Divergence: Codex family weighted the funding-cutoff+deadline forcing function (0.72-0.74); Opus family weighted the outsider dig-in dynamic (0.45-0.60). Family divergence 0.205 > 0.10 -> engine auto-skips, FLAG FOR HUMAN. Note all 4 estimators are BELOW market (0.91), i.e. ensemble thinks dropout is overpriced, but the two families disagree too much to trade.

items.title: The Odyssey (Nolan) Rotten Tomatoes Tomatometer above 90 on Monday after wide release?
items.rationale: Core: Nolan base rate ~5/12 films strictly above 90; uniformly glowing early reactions ('one of his best', Best Picture frontrunner) push above base rate. Risk: strictly >90 excludes the crowded 88-92 cluster; early social mini-reviews don't feed RT and are positive-biased. Round-2 crux (revealed to estimators): settlement is only ~3 days post-release (07-20), so first-wave-review inflation modestly favors a higher score -> both families moved up (half-anchor capped) from blind 0.555 to finals 0.58/0.592, still below market ~0.64. Disagreement point: whether the 3-day timing inflation offsets the knife-edge threshold.

items.title: Will the Fed maintain rates (hike 0bps) at the July 29, 2026 FOMC meeting?
items.rationale: Core: high base rate for no-change at any given meeting; CME FedWatch (~73% hold as of 07-07) and Kalshi ladder concentrate residual risk on a 25bp HIKE (hawkish regime), not a cut; 22 days out with no scheduled CPI/PCE/speeches in the given evidence. Consensus 0.828 sits essentially AT market (~0.84) -> no edge, no trade. Reported for calibration; the ensemble correctly declines to force a trade on the most efficiently-priced macro market.
